texto: O Brahmana, it is just like a mountain river, flowing far and swift, taking everything along with it; there is no moment, no instant, no second when it stops flowing, but goes on flowing and continuing. So Brahmana, is human life, like a mountain river." As Buddha told Ratthpala : "The world is a continuous flux and is impermanent.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Walpola Ruhula: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de passagem do tempo e prioridades. O trecho “O Brahmana, it is just like a mountain river, flowing far and swift, taking everything along with…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que tempo, buddhism, philosophy pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de philosophy.

frase_original: O Brahmana, it is just like a mountain river, flowing far and swift, taking everything along with it; there is no moment, no instant, no second when it stops flowing, but goes on flowing and continuing. So Brahmana, is human life, like a mountain river." As Buddha told Ratthpala : "The world is a continuous flux and is impermanent.
